§6Ferric Schrabidate§f

§aAn iron-schrabidium alloy — the precursor to dineutronium and end-game materials!§f

§bFerric Schrabidate§f is an alloy between iron and schrabidium in the form of schrabidic acid. It behaves like a radioactive material with the added Schrabidic property of causing blindness when held.

§6Production§f (§bChemical Plant§f):
§b• 1×§f Iron Powder
§b• 250 mB§f Schrabidic Acid
§b→ §eFerric Schrabidate Powder§f

§bSmelt§f the powder into an ingot normally.

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• Dineutronium§f — primary ingredient, processed in the §bParticle Exposure Chamber§f
§b• Storage Block§f — for compact storage
§b• Anvil§f — Ferric Schrabidate Anvil
§b• Dense Wire§f — for high-end electrical components
§b• CMB-Schrabidate Laser Crystal§f — enables UV range for FEL

§eTip§f: You'll need significant quantities of ferric schrabidate for dineutronium production. Set up a steady schrabidium → schrabidic acid → ferric schrabidate pipeline before attempting the Particle Accelerator!
